Title: Pinoresinol rescues developmental phenotypes of Arabidopsis phenylpropanoid mutants overexpressing FERULATE 5-HYDROXYLASE
Journal: Proceedings of the National Academy of Sciences of the United States of America

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: 5-hydroxylates guaiacyl-type phenylpropanoid intermediates, redirecting flux from coniferyl alcohol-derived guaiacyl products toward sinapyl alcohol/syringyl products
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: Coniferyl alcohol-derived guaiacyl precursors; coniferaldehyde/coniferyl alcohol pool
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: 5-hydroxylated intermediates leading to sinapaldehyde and sinapyl alcohol
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Competes with and diverts flux away from coniferyl alcohol/pinoresinol toward syringyl monolignol formation
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key control point for G/S lignin composition
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Overexpressed from the C4H promoter in phenylpropanoid tissues
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: C4H promoter; phenylpropanoid-associated/inducible in relevant tissues
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Supports normal plant growth, lateral root development, and root hair morphology
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Overexpression in ref3, ccr1, cadc, cadd, and cadc cadd backgrounds causes severe growth impairment, reduced lateral roots, and altered root hair morphology, consistent with depletion of coniferyl alcohol-derived growth-promoting metabolite(s) including pinoresinol
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Genetic overexpression in multiple mutant backgrounds; mutant comparisons with and without F5H; targeted metabolite profiling; chemical complementation with coniferyl alcohol and pinoresinol; lateral root phenotyping
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Exogenous coniferyl alcohol; pinoresinol and related lignans/neolignans; NAA auxin treatment
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Untargeted LC-MS metabolomics; stable-isotope labeling with [12C6]/[13C6] coniferyl alcohol; PODIUM LC/MS pipeline; LC-MS/MS
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Manipulation of F5H to alter lignin composition should be paired with maintenance of coniferyl alcohol/lignan pools to avoid lignin-modification-induced dwarfism
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Severe dwarfism, sterility in some mutant backgrounds, fewer lateral roots, abnormal root hairs
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: F5H overexpression alone mainly shifts lignin toward syringyl units with mild growth effects, but in phenylpropanoid-deficient backgrounds it exacerbates defects; complementation is not explained by large suppression of transgene expression
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. F5H overexpression in multiple phenylpropanoid mutants caused dwarfism and developmental defects. 2. These defects were rescued by exogenous coniferyl alcohol and especially pinoresinol. 3. The data support a model in which elevated F5H drains coniferyl alcohol-derived metabolite pools required for growth.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Reduces hydroxycinnamaldehydes such as coniferaldehyde and sinapaldehyde to the corresponding monolignol alcohols
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Directly forms coniferyl alcohol precursor pool required for pinoresinol synthesis
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Primary CAD involved in floral stem lignin biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Supports plant growth and lateral root development
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Loss of CADC together with CADD and F5H overexpression causes severe developmental defects linked to insufficient coniferyl alcohol-derived metabolite production; single cadc with F5H overexpression also reduces lateral roots
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Mutant analysis of cadc, cadd, and cadc cadd with/without C4H:F5H; metabolite quantification; chemical rescue by coniferyl alcohol and pinoresinol
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Exogenous coniferyl alcohol and pinoresinol treatments
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Targeted metabolite profiling; untargeted LC-MS metabolomics
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Maintaining CAD activity is important when engineering lignin composition to preserve growth-promoting coniferyl alcohol-derived metabolites
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Combined deficiency with F5H overexpression causes dwarfism and root defects
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: cadc cadd still accumulates some coniferyl alcohol, implying additional Arabidopsis CADs contribute to residual activity
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CADC is one of the primary enzymes supplying coniferyl alcohol for downstream lignan-related growth functions. 2. Defects become especially severe when combined with CADD loss and F5H overexpression. 3. Chemical complementation implicates deficient coniferyl alcohol-derived metabolites rather than lignin quantity alone.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Reduces hydroxycinnamaldehydes to hydroxycinnamyl alcohols, contributing to coniferyl and sinapyl alcohol biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Directly forms coniferyl alcohol precursor pool required for pinoresinol synthesis
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Primary CAD involved in floral stem lignin biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Supports plant growth, lateral root emergence, and root hair morphology
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: cadd C4H:F5H has reduced lateral roots and defective root hairs; cadc cadd C4H:F5H is severely dwarfed, and both defects are rescued by coniferyl alcohol/pinoresinol
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Mutant analysis; root and shoot phenotyping; cryo-SEM; chemical complementation with coniferyl alcohol, pinoresinol, and methylated pinoresinol derivatives
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Exogenous coniferyl alcohol; pinoresinol; MMPR; DMPR; NAA
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Targeted metabolite profiling; untargeted LC-MS metabolomics
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Avoid simultaneous CAD impairment and aggressive F5H-driven lignin engineering if normal development is required
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Reduced lateral roots, branched root hairs, severe dwarfism in double-mutant plus F5H-overexpression background
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Lateral root defect in cadd C4H:F5H is rescued by pinoresinol and even by methylated pinoresinol derivatives that cannot be incorporated into lignin, supporting a signaling/bioactive role
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CADD loss reveals dependence of root and shoot development on coniferyl alcohol-derived metabolites under F5H overexpression. 2. cadd C4H:F5H shows lateral root and root-hair defects. 3. Rescue by pinoresinol derivatives argues for lignan deficiency rather than lignin incorporation defect.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Reduces hydroxycinnamoyl-CoA thioesters to the corresponding hydroxycinnamaldehydes
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Supplies precursor pool for coniferyl alcohol and downstream pinoresinol biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: ccr1 plants overexpressing F5H display decreased rosette area, reduced height, and sterility, consistent with depletion of coniferyl alcohol-derived growth-promoting metabolites
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Mutant plus transgene analysis; growth phenotyping; targeted metabolite profiling
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Targeted metabolite profiling; untargeted LC-MS metabolomics
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: CCR1 impairment combined with F5H-based lignin engineering can impose strong developmental penalties
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Dwarfism and sterility when combined with F5H overexpression
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: ccr1 alone has mild phenotype, but F5H overexpression strongly aggravates growth defects
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. F5H overexpression converts mildly affected ccr1 plants into strongly growth-impaired sterile plants. 2. Metabolite shifts support depletion of coniferyl alcohol-derived pools. 3. The phenotype supports the model that non-lignin phenylpropanoids promote growth.

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Entry pathway to monolignols and related phenylpropanoids
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Hydroxylates cinnamic acid to p-coumaric acid in the second step of the phenylpropanoid pathway
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Supplies the general precursor pool for coniferyl alcohol and pinoresinol biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Promoter used to drive F5H overexpression construct
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Supports growth and root development via downstream phenylpropanoid products
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: ref3 mutant alleles overexpressing F5H become more dwarf, and ref3-2 C4H:F5H growth can be partially rescued by coniferyl alcohol, implicating C4H-supplied precursor flux in production of growth-promoting coniferyl alcohol derivatives
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: ref3 mutant analysis with/without F5H overexpression; growth phenotyping; chemical complementation with coniferyl alcohol
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Targeted metabolite profiling; untargeted LC-MS metabolomics
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Strong reduction in early phenylpropanoid flux risks developmental defects, especially if downstream flux is further redirected by F5H
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Dwarfism and severe growth impairment when combined with F5H overexpression
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The C4H promoter also drove the F5H transgene; CA treatment did not materially suppress endogenous C4H expression under the tested conditions
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Partial C4H deficiency sensitizes plants to F5H overexpression. 2. The aggravated phenotype is alleviated by coniferyl alcohol. 3. Early phenylpropanoid flux is therefore required for production of a coniferyl alcohol-derived growth factor, likely including pinoresinol.
